title: cruciata neotaurica
intro: Cruciata neotaurica, also known as the marsh peppercress or crosswort, is a perennial herb that belongs to the Rubiaceae family and is commonly found in wetlands and aquatic environments. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Cruciata neotaurica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infused in oil, or made into a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
